channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Confusing road markings, 5th March
item.description:
Road markings on Fortis Green Road traffic lights approaching junction. LH lane has marking for LH turn and straight on. RH marking has straight on marking as no RH turn allowed. LH lane should only be for LH turn into Fortis Green. Currently two cars can dangerously attempt to cross straight on
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 230, Fortis Green Road, Muswell Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Continious water leaking, 5th March
item.description:
There is water continuously leaking from the pavement/drain outside my property in Bromley. It appears to be coming from underground rather than rainwater.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakham Drive, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car left with flat tyre for months, 5th March
item.description:
Abandoned with flat tyre

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 23, Hampden Road, Colney Hatch, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip and litter, 5th March
item.description:
Pram dumped by lockers on Faversham Road. Now there for 4 days, despite being previously reported. Also, overnight build up of rubbish discarded from lockers and items bought in shop. Street requires sweeping again.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Faversham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 5th March
item.description:
Items left on footpath. This is now the main footpath used by pedestrians as the footpath opposite has been taken over by 28 Bellegrove Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Bellegrove Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 63 Cricklewood Broadway in Keyes Road, 5th March
item.description:
Mattress, machine, shopping trolley & bags

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Keyes Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: All along Dawes Street, 5th March
item.description:
Can someone please litter pick this road not been done for days
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dawes Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Goldsmiths Row overflowing with rubbish, 5th March
item.description:
Bins overflowing on Goldsmiths Row for days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Goldsmiths Row, London

item.title: Danson Park play area, 5th March
item.description:
The play park is very dangerous due to the following issues 2 climbing nets on a multi play unit are broken and need replacing. There are sharp spikes on the nets that need taking off On the multi play unit there is a rotten post at that the bottom and a bolt is sticking through it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Park, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Many more potholes, 5th March
item.description:
Road deteriorating rapidly
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Petersham Drive, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on Tabard street, 5th March
item.description:
There are plastic bottles, cans, paper and food packaging on the pavement alongside the park and road on Tabard Street. It has been there for three days.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tabard Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Obstruction, 5th March
item.description:
Hi, I hope you are well. My name is Andrea Turner, and I am the location manager at Crown Meadow Court, 23 Brosse Way, Bromley, Kent, which is a care home. We provide extra care for 62 residents and regularly receive ambulances, doctors, and deliveries. However, the way our car park is set up means that ambulances and other vehicles cannot exit easily without driving onto the pavement or risking damage. Cars parked opposite often block the entrance, leaving us stuck. This has caused a lot of damage to vehicles, including an ambulance that had its tyres damaged yesterday. We would appreciate your support in resolving this issue. We would like to know how we can address this problem, as cars often park across the entrance. across the road. which prevents us from turning out of the turning. We hope it is possible to install yellow box lines. Kind regards, Andrea Turne
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rookery Lane, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trip hazard, 5th March
item.description:
I reported previously and the wrong paving slab was removed and tarmac laid. I received a reply to advise issue resolve but it hasn’t. I have highlighted 2 paving slabs that keep raising when vehicles park up on the verge. Due to no support on the verge (earth has worn away), the paving slabs keep raising up and I have to continually stamp them down in position to prevent someone tripping. I have already put back down in position to prevent someone tripping.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ashcroft Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish in Lower Marvels Wood, 5th March
item.description:
Is Lower Marvels Wood common dump? Household rubbish and garden rubbish in Lower Marvels Wood. Location: Side entrance next to 7 Duddington Close.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dunkery Road, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter spill, 5th March

Litter spill /flytip?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kemerton Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass verges damaged by contractors, 5th March
item.description:
Grass verges all along Wickham Way have been cut when too wet and cut perhaps too close. Now damaged and muddy. Also, cuttings dispersed in road and on pavements have not been cleared up. Potential drain blocking/clogging. And messy.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 59, Wickham Way, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There is a very bad word written in the cement on the road!, 5th March
item.description:
Someone has written a swear word in the cement on the road by the side of the fence and now my daughter is learning to read it caused a very awkward conversation! Can it please be filled in/covered with something? Sorry about the content in the photo but had to share what it looks like!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Groveland Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous tree, 5th March
item.description:
Tree encroaching my property causing mess, damage to cars and slip hazard on the pavement when fruit falls from tree during fruit fall season . Fruit fall on cars causing stains on car paint work, mess on the drive and squashed fruit gets trodden into house. This has been ongoing for 2 years and as a council tax payer I want this tree problem sorted . May be i should start billing the council for cleaning up the mess from this tree . Also the tree fruit is a slip hazard on pavement . Please reply ,not just a automated reply. Steve kent
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eden Way, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
